items.correct: The adaptation of global products or services to local tastes, regulations or cultures — e.g. McDonald's offering paneer burgers in India or Tesco selling local foods in different countries.
items.misconception: Glocalisation means the destruction of local cultures by global brands.
items.random_wrong: Glocalisation is a typo of globalisation.
items.why: TNCs glocalise to maximise local sales; it's a strategic response, not cultural homogenisation. Students sometimes describe globalisation effects as glocalisation, missing the active local-adaptation element.

items.q: What is the main criticism of Rostow's stages of economic growth?
items.correct: It assumes all countries can follow the same linear Western industrialisation path, ignoring colonial legacies, global trade inequalities and environmental constraints — a top-down modernisation view.
items.misconception: All LICs will pass through Rostow's five stages if they wait long enough.
items.random_wrong: Rostow's model is universally accepted as the best development theory.
items.why: Rostow assumed Western industrialisation as a template, but global economic structures may prevent LICs replicating it. Dependency theorists argue underdevelopment is actively produced — not just a delay.

items.correct: The combined loss of water from a surface through evaporation (from soil and water bodies) and transpiration (from plant leaves).
items.misconception: It is only water evaporating from puddles and lakes.
items.random_wrong: It is the process of rain falling from clouds.
items.why: Students focus only on evaporation. Transpiration from forests can move enormous quantities of water — a key concept for understanding deforestation's effect on the water cycle.
It's Human Development Index. “High Development Indicator” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Norway. “UK” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many stages in Rostow's model?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 stages. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
